meaning: adj.不标准的,(尤指语言)不规范的,[数]非标准的
examples: Blockchain technology in recording farmers' actions is widely used to avoid nonstandard pesticide use.
The CREATE statement has one slightly nonstandard bit of SQL syntax.
Numbered enumerations invite nonstandard extensions that do not interoperate.
Liddy has also fled the sinkhole called nonstandard auto insurance--policies for bad drivers.
Almost all Japanese houses are individually commissioned by the owner and constructed by small firms using nonstandard materials and methods.
